relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file represents a lending decision override approval from risk_and_compliance to credit; the outbound file represents loan disbursement instructions from credit to accounts. This forms a logical pipeline: an override approval triggers disbursement preparation. Both files share loan identifiers, customer/borrower concepts, loan amounts, currency, and loan terms, indicating data lineage from approval to execution.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for the disbursement instruction; no source in inbound schema.
Inbound loanApplicationId becomes the loan_id in the outbound disbursement instruction.
Borrower legal name is not present in inbound schema; likely retrieved from customer master data in credit system.
Account number is not present in inbound schema; likely retrieved from customer banking details in credit system.
Inbound loanAmount directly maps to outbound disbursement_amount as the principal to be disbursed.
Currency code is copied directly from inbound loan details to outbound disbursement instruction.
Scheduled disbursement date is not present in inbound schema; likely calculated or set by credit system based on approval date and processing rules.
Disbursement method is not specified in inbound schema; likely a default or system-determined value based on loan product type.
Destination account number is not present in inbound schema; likely retrieved from customer banking details in credit system.
Bank identifier code is not present in inbound schema; likely retrieved from customer banking details in credit system.
Loan purpose is not present in inbound schema; likely stored separately in credit system loan details or derived from product type.
Inbound loanTerm (in months) maps directly to outbound loan_term_months.
Interest rate is not present in inbound schema; likely determined by credit system based on risk assessment and product terms.
Inbound overrideDecision value 'APPROVED' maps to outbound status 'APPROVED', indicating the disbursement instruction inherits approval status from the override.
Credit officer who created the disbursement instruction is not present in inbound schema; likely a credit system user, not from the override message.
Inbound message timestamp may serve as the basis for created_date, though the actual creation date in credit system may differ slightly.
Inbound approvedBy (risk and compliance executive) maps to outbound approved_by for the disbursement instruction.
Inbound approvalDate maps to outbound approved_date as the date the disbursement was approved.
Inbound conditions array may be serialized or summarized into outbound remarks field as approval conditions or processing notes.
